The '''resumptive''' aspect in Aelaki expresses the resumption or restarting of a previously ceased action or state. It is one of the [[Stative aspects|stative verb aspects]], formed with the prefix '''lu-'''.

== Formation ==

The resumptive is formed by prefixing '''lu-''' to the '''umlaut''' (gnomic) stem of the stative verb.

{| class="wikitable"
! Stative verb !! Resumptive !! Meaning
|-
| zoduk (know) || luzedik || remember (resume knowing)
|-
| apafath (fall) || lu'apafath || resume falling
|}

Note that the resumptive uses the umlaut stem ''zedik'' rather than the base stem ''zoduk''.

== Continuative form ==

The continuative of the resumptive is formed by reduplicating the prefix:

{| class="wikitable"
! Base !! Continuative !! Meaning
|-
| luzedik || luluzedik || remembering (in the process of resuming knowing)
|}

== Semantic scope ==

The resumptive indicates that a state which had stopped is now starting again. With ''zoduk'' "know", the resumptive ''luzedik'' means "remember" -- that is, to resume knowing something that had been forgotten ([[Cessative|cessative]] ''nïzedik'').

== Relationship to other stative aspects ==

{| class="wikitable"
! Prefix !! Aspect !! Example !! Meaning
|-
| ho- || [[Probable]] || hozoduk || curious (about to know)
|-
| nü- || [[Inchoative]] || nüzoduk || learn (start to know)
|-
| nï- (+ umlaut) || [[Cessative]] || nïzedik || forget (stop knowing)
|-
| '''lu-''' || '''Resumptive''' || '''luzedik''' || '''remember (resume knowing)'''
|-
| ko- || [[Almost]] || kozoduk || almost learn
|}

== See also ==

* [[Cessative]]
* [[Inchoative]]
* [[Repetitive]]

[[Category:grammatical aspects]]
[[Category:Stative aspects]]
[[Category:git synced pages]]
